reason: Response A is a genuine, warmly written personal note with emotional depth, natural pacing, and conversational authenticity - the kind of thoughtful writing a mid-tier Sonnet model would produce. Response B is not actually a note at all; it is a meta-description of what a note would contain, which reads like a lower-capability model (Haiku) either misunderstanding the task or producing a truncated/confused output rather than fulfilling the request directly.
